Production Approach
& Budget Direction

June 2026 — Private Founder Memo

We spent the last week evaluating locations, speaking with international production partners, pressure-testing both creative routes and building an execution plan that balances creative ambition with production practicality.

Scroll
01The Creative Routes

Two films. Two distinct emotional registers.

Each route is a complete idea in its own right — built to carry the brand on a global stage while staying unmistakably in open form.

ROUTE 01

Hey
Snowflakes

A whimsical road film about a man who keeps postponing the plan and lets the journey decide instead. Strangers, detours and small collisions of chance accumulate — and arrive, finally, as snowfall in a place that has no business holding it.

Key Creative Themes
Freedom Serendipity Open Journeys Unexpected Connections
Production Scale High
ROUTE 02

Cotton
Ball

The brand launch film. A surreal world where the clothing rewrites everything it touches — colour, texture, gravity, mood. Cotton becomes the language of the film, soft and total, until the wardrobe and the world are speaking the same sentence.

Key Creative Themes
Surrealism Texture Imagination Visual Metaphor
Production Scale High

03Production Pathways

One vision, two ways to execute it.

After evaluating multiple possibilities, we structured the execution into two pathways — each with its own rhythm of recce, prep and shoot.

05Photography

Photography Treatment

Alongside the films, we have also considered a stylised photography treatment that aligns with the overall visual language of the campaign. The objective is to create a cohesive content ecosystem across motion, stills and social communication while maintaining consistency in tone, styling and storytelling.

0
Retouched Stills

A complete stills suite — approximately five frames per scene across both films, retouched and delivered in 16:9 and 9:16 for editorial, lookbook and social use.

Editorial Fashion Campaign Luxury Lookbook Premium Brand Launch
06Deliverables

A content ecosystem, not a single film.

Every asset is designed to be maximised across launch, seasonal drops and ongoing brand communication — relevant and usable over an extended period.

0
Brand Films
0
Content Deliverables
0
Retouched Stills
0
BTS Films

Films & Cutdowns

  • 2 ×Master films, 90–120s — 16:9 & 9:16
  • 4 ×30s cutdowns (intro, scenes, outro)
  • 4 ×30s teaser & promo assets

Social Adaptations

  • 12 ×20s adaptations for Reels
  • 12 ×10s adaptations for Reels
  • 12 ×6s adaptations for Reels

Stills & BTS

  • 60 ×Retouched stills — 5 per scene
  • 6 ×BTS films, 25–30s (3 per film)
  • All ×Delivered in 16:9 & 9:16 ratios

08Our Observations
01

If the objective is a globally rooted visual identity, the international route offers stronger visual differentiation.

02

If the objective is production control and operational flexibility, India presents a highly efficient execution pathway.

03

Both routes remain creatively viable. The decision is one of intent, not compromise.

04

A hybrid production model may also be considered — one route executed internationally, the other within India.

This allows flexibility in balancing visual ambition, production requirements and final budget considerations, while still achieving a cohesive campaign outcome.

09Important Considerations

The fine print, kept honest.

A few dependencies worth holding in view as we move toward locking a route and a window.

The travel, accommodation and logistics costs currently included within the budget ranges are indicative estimates based on prevailing rates. Final costs may vary depending on the time of booking, travel dates, crew size and location selected.

Both routes are heavily dependent on outdoor locations and natural environments, so we recommend carrying a weather contingency from the planning stage.

Depending on the final window and prevailing conditions, additional contingency may be needed for delays, location shifts or extra shoot days. We monitor patterns closely and build the final schedule accordingly.

A lean leadership team travels from Mumbai — Director, Director's Assistant and Producer — to ensure continuity of vision across both films.

The wider crew ecosystem (cinematography support, local production, camera, lighting, grip, equipment and logistics) is sourced through experienced regional partners for efficiency and control.

Figures shown are indicative and prepared to help evaluate the overall scale and feasibility of the campaign. Currency conversion is calculated at a locked rate at the time of confirmation.

A detailed line-item budget, confirmed crew structure and final execution methodology follow once the preferred route is finalised.

Final location selection depends on the route lock and weather during the proposed window. A location-cluster strategy is developed during recce to minimise company moves and maximise shooting efficiency.

Certain creative beats carry their own dependencies — casting availability, the hero convertible and rigging for Route 01, and custom fabrication and safety review for the Cotton world in Route 02.

open ended

The idea is not to arrive.
The idea is to keep moving.
